title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 20, 2026: Navigating Geopolitical Tensions and Key Earnings

items.headline: U.S. Futures Edge Higher Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Upcoming Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed modest gains this Monday morning, signaling a cautious start to the week as investors weigh escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East against a busy schedule of major tech earnings. Futures for the Nasdaq 100 rose 1.00%, S&P 500 futures increased by 0.49%, and Dow Jones futures advanced 0.34% in pre-market trading. The market remains sensitive to ongoing U.S. airstrikes on Iran and volatile oil prices, which continue to fuel inflation concerns. Sentiment is also shaped by renewed fears surrounding an AI bubble, especially after a significant sell-off in semiconductor stocks last week.

items.headline: AMC Soars on Record Quarterly Performance
items.summary: AMC Entertainment Holdings, Inc. ($AMC) saw its stock soar nearly 15% in off-hours trading after reporting record second-quarter sales and profit, surpassing analyst expectations.

items.headline: Domino's Pizza Jumps on Strong Revenue
items.summary: Domino's Pizza Inc. ($DPZ) jumped over 7% after beating second-quarter revenue projections and announcing a new round of stock buybacks, despite falling short of profit targets.

items.headline: Nvidia Rebounds in Early Trading
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) rose 1.6% early Monday, as chip companies show signs of swinging back upward after a recent sell-off.

items.headline: MV Oil Trust Plunges on Final Distribution
items.summary: MV Oil Trust ($MVO) experienced the biggest decline in July, falling over 52%, following the announcement of its final trust distribution.

items.headline: Alarum Technologies Down Amidst Inquiry Response
items.summary: Alarum Technologies Ltd. ($ALAR) dropped over 51% in July 2026, responding to an inquiry into residential proxy networks.

items.headline: IBM Stock Crashes on Q2 Miss
items.summary: International Business Machines ($IBM) saw its stock crash by 24% last week after a significant Q2 earnings miss, contributing to broader tech sector declines.

items.headline: Energy Leads as Technology Falters in Major Sector Rotation
items.summary: A significant sector rotation is underway, with Energy emerging as the leading sector, buoyed by rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions. Conversely, the Technology sector has been the worst performer, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index entering bear market territory amid waning AI trade momentum and concerns over elevated valuations.

items.headline: Big Tech Earnings Set to Dominate the Week's Agenda
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting a slew of earnings reports this week from major technology companies, including Tesla ($TSLA), Alphabet ($GOOGL), and Intel ($INTC). Their performance will be crucial in determining market direction and the sustainability of the AI-driven rally.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Volatility and Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Escalating U.S. airstrikes on Iran and heightened geopolitical risks in the Middle East continue to impact global oil prices, with Brent crude recently crossing $90 per barrel. This volatility is reigniting inflation concerns and adding an element of uncertainty to the market outlook.

items.headline: June Inflation Data Shows Mixed Signals
items.summary: Recent inflation data indicates a complex picture. In Canada, lower gas prices helped bring the annual inflation rate down to 2.8% in June. Meanwhile, in the U.S., the Consumer Price Index for June showed annual inflation at 3.5%, still above the Federal Reserve's target, despite a monthly drop in CPI and PPI.
